vulnerabilities.name: CAPTCHA Protection Enabled
vulnerabilities.description: The application uses Advanced Dynamic CAPTCHA (GUID Tokenized) to protect the login endpoint. The authentication process is 'Protected against automation'.
vulnerabilities.impact: Automated brute-force and credential stuffing attacks are effectively mitigated. The CAPTCHA ensures that each login attempt requires human verification or complex bypass techniques.
vulnerabilities.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Observe the CAPTCHA image, widget, or specific endpoint (/CaptchaImage.axd).
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: Detected Advanced Dynamic CAPTCHA (GUID Tokenized) at http://localhost:8081/login. Source indicators: Advanced Dynamic CAPTCHA (GUID Tokenized).
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Brute-force attack not feasible due to active CAPTCHA. Manual testing or CAPTCHA bypass required for further testing.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix: N/A - Defensive controls are active and functioning as intended.

vulnerabilities.name: ASP.NET State Management Detected
vulnerabilities.description: The application uses ASP.NET state management tokens (__VIEWSTATE, __EVENTVALIDATION, etc.) for session and integrity control.
vulnerabilities.impact: Increases the complexity for basic automated tools and provides a baseline for CSRF protection.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Ensure VIEWSTATE is signed (MAC) and encrypted to prevent manipulation.

vulnerabilities.name: Client-Side Obfuscation Detected
vulnerabilities.description: The application uses client-side JavaScript (e.g., Process(), asmcrypto.js) to process or obfuscate data before submission.
vulnerabilities.impact: Provides a layer of obfuscation that can hinder basic automation and network-level cleartext inspection.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Do not rely on client-side obfuscation for security. Ensure robust server-side hashing and HTTPS enforcement.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix: Always use strong server-side hashing (Argon2/BCrypt) and enforce TLS for all authentication traffic.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: Permissions-Policy
vulnerabilities.description: The security-enhancing header 'Permissions-Policy' is not present in the HTTP response.
vulnerabilities.impact: Attackers can exploit the lack of browser-side security controls to perform XSS, clickjacking, or data leakage.
vulnerabilities.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send a GET request to http://localhost:8081/login.
2. Inspect headers in the response.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: Header 'Permissions-Policy' missing from response at http://localhost:8081/login
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Define a Permissions-Policy to restrict browser features like camera or geolocation.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix:
In Nginx: add_header Permissions-Policy 'value';
In Flask: response.headers['Permissions-Policy'] = 'value'

vulnerabilities.name: Exposed Administrative Panel
vulnerabilities.description: An administrative login or dashboard was discovered at a common path.
vulnerabilities.impact: Provides a direct target for brute-force attacks and unauthorized administrative access if weak credentials are used.
vulnerabilities.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Navigate to http://localhost:8081/login/admin.
2. Observe the administrative login interface.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Restrict access to the admin panel using IP whitelisting or a VPN. Change the default /admin path to a non-obvious URL.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix: Nginx: allow 192.168.1.0/24; deny all; inside the admin location block.

vulnerabilities.name: Interesting Path Discovered: /login
vulnerabilities.description: A resource was found at /login which may contain sensitive information or internal logic.
vulnerabilities.impact: Information disclosure that assists in further reconnaissance.
vulnerabilities.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Request http://localhost:8081/login/login.
2. Inspect the content for internal details.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review the necessity of this path being public. If not required, disable access or require authentication.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix: Options -Indexes in Apache or autoindex off in Nginx to prevent directory listing.

vulnerabilities.name: Exposed Git Repository
vulnerabilities.description: The .git directory is accessible, allowing attackers to download the entire source code history and internal configurations.
vulnerabilities.impact: Source code theft, exposure of hardcoded secrets, and full reverse engineering of the application logic.
vulnerabilities.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Access http://localhost:8081/login/.git/config or http://localhost:8081/login/.git/index in a browser.
2. Use 'git-dumper' to pull the full repository.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: Exposed git directory found at http://localhost:8081/login/.git (HTTP 302)
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Block access to the .git directory immediately and clean any leaked secrets from the repository history before redeploying.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix:
Nginx: location ~ /\.git { deny all; }
Apache: <Directory ~ "/\.git"> Order allow,deny Deny from all </Directory>

vulnerabilities.name: Exposed Environment File (.env)
vulnerabilities.description: The .env file containing sensitive environment variables like DB credentials and API keys is publicly accessible.
vulnerabilities.impact: Immediate full system compromise. Attackers can access databases, third-party services, and cloud infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send a GET request to http://localhost:8081/login/.env.
2. Read sensitive keys like DB_PASSWORD or AWS_SECRET.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: Successfully accessed http://localhost:8081/login/.env with content length 166
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Move the .env file outside of the web root and rotate all secrets contained within it immediately.
vulnerabilities.technical_fix: Move .env to a parent directory not served by the web server. Use environment variables injected by the OS or Docker instead of a file.
